Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: We begin with a brief review of its development history, starting from the initial

Composition_Info.Note: This is a software package paper describing GPUMD 4.0, a molecular dynamics simulation package with native machine-learned potentials (NEP). No experimental material compositions are reported; this is a methodology/software review article.

Process_Text.original: GPUMD 4.0 released April 2025; NEP4 developed 2021-2024; contains ~85,000 lines of CUDA C++ source code; supports heterogeneous CPU-GPU computing; nep executable for NEP model training; gpumd executable for MD simulations
Process_Text.simplified: GPUMD 4.0 software package with NEP machine-learned potential framework for atomistic MD simulations

Microstructure_Text.original: Software_Description: GPUMD 4.0 is a molecular dynamics package for heterogeneous CPU-GPU computing platforms; NEP_Approach: Neuroevolution Potential - combines Behler-Parinello neural network methodology with custom descriptor and training approach; Capabilities: Supports empirical potentials (LJ, EAM, Tersoff, ILP), machine-learned potentials (NEP, FCP, Deep Potential), various integrators/ensembles (NVE, NVT, NPT, NPH), path-integral MD, shock simulations, hybrid MC-MD
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Software_Description: GPUMD 4.0 is a molecular dynamics package for heterogeneous CPU-GPU computing platforms; NEP_Approach: Neuroevolution Potential - combines Behler-Parinello neural network methodology with custom descriptor and training approach; Capabilities: Supports empirical potentials (LJ, EAM, Tersoff, ILP), machine-learned potentials (NEP, FCP, Deep Potential), various integrators/ensembles (NVE, NVT, NPT, NPH), path-integral MD, shock simulations, hybrid MC-MD

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: NEP on V100 GPU for bulk Si
Properties_Info.Note: NEP computational speed; 15x faster than Deep Potential (DP) on same hardware

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Periodic table coverage for inorganic and organic materials
Properties_Info.Note: First NEP-based foundation model covering virtually entire periodic table
